response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong craftsmanship credentials (TDD, testing tools, open-source work), but the profile does not meet this role's core minimum qualifications. The position requires 8 years of C++; the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~7 months) plus a thesis project, with a career centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. High-scale systems exposure exists (Sociomantic's millions of requests/second), but it is roughly two years rather than five, and there is no evidence of leading technical strategy for a cloud compute platform or formally mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in a leadership capacity.
strengths: Genuine high-throughput, low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second)
Strong engineering-practice foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus authored testing frameworks
Demonstrated mentoring inclination via workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
Limited large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems tenure (~2 years) versus the 5 required
No formal technical-leadership role owning platform strategy or leading cross-team alignment

key_factors.factor: Low-latency distributed systems work at scale
